events.title: Busan container rates rise sharply, pushing KCCI up 4.6%
events.description: Container spot rates from Busan have increased across most trade lanes, leading to a 4.62% rise in the KOBC Container Composite Index (KCCI), which now stands at 3,920 points.

events.title: Indian sailors avoid Gulf due to Middle East conflict
events.description: Indian seafarers, a major global workforce in merchant shipping, are hesitant to return to the Gulf amid the Middle East war, causing potential labor shortages and operational disruptions in shipping.

events.title: ARA bunker fuel availability tight
events.description: Bunker fuel availability is very tight in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) region, with buyers advised to book HSFO and VLSFO supplies up to seven days in advance to avoid high premiums.

events.title: Stranded ships in Persian Gulf decrease after evacuation
events.description: The number of container ships stranded in the Persian Gulf has dropped to 80 vessels (170,000 TEU) from a peak of 138 due to the US-Israel-Iran conflict. The International Maritime Organization's evacuation plan has helped more than 40% of ships escape.

events.title: EU imposes 3 euro customs fee on small parcel imports
events.description: Starting July 1, the EU is imposing a 3 euro customs fee on small parcel imports, prompting retailers to adjust systems for compliance and potentially affecting consumer behavior due to higher costs.

events.title: U.S. rail systems slow due to rising intermodal volume
events.description: Increased intermodal volume, driven by rising fuel and trucking costs, is causing delays at major U.S. rail systems. The surge is impacting freight flow efficiency across the country.

events.title: USMCA renewal uncertainty impacts North American trade
events.description: The U.S. has rejected automatic renewal of the USMCA trade agreement, leading to annual negotiations and creating uncertainty for North America’s $1.5 trillion trading relationship.

events.title: US rail slowdowns due to intermodal volume surge
events.description: US intermodal rail volume increased by 10% last week, but the surge is causing major network slowdowns with train speeds at 10-20 month lows. This contrasts with declining volumes in Canada, highlighting growing operational challenges in North American rail freight.

events.title: Extreme Heat Impacts Reefer Capacity in Eastern US
events.description: Extreme heat across the Eastern US is causing reefer spot rates and tender rejections to reach near all-time highs, as companies shift from dry van to refrigerated transport to manage temperature-sensitive freight.

events.title: U.S. House GOP urges Jones Act waivers to expire
events.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republican leaders are urging President Trump to let Jones Act shipping waivers expire in August, which could impact domestic maritime freight operations.

events.title: Armed Boarders Damage Merchant Ship Off Yemen
events.description: A merchant vessel was illegally boarded and damaged by armed personnel off the coast of Yemen. The UK Maritime Trade Operations issued a fresh security warning following the incident.

events.title: Hong Kong Port Restricted Area for Special Event
events.description: A restricted area is in place off Stonecutters Island, Hong Kong, from July 2 to July 6, 2026, for a special event. Only government launches and event-related vessels are permitted.

events.title: Container ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A foreign container ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz while using an unapproved route, according to Iranian state TV. The incident highlights tensions over control of the strategically vital waterway.

events.title: Pirate Boarding of Bulk Carrier in Gulf of Aden
events.description: A bulk carrier was boarded by pirates armed with a rocket-propelled grenade launcher approximately 110 nautical miles northeast of Bosaso, Somalia. The incident occurred in the Internationally Recommended Transit Corridor.

events.title: Containership Grounding Near Hormuz Strait
events.description: A containership reportedly went aground near the Strait of Hormuz for operating outside its designated shipping lane. Iranian state media released a video and is using the incident to assert control over the critical waterway.

events.title: Russian warship orders Greenpeace away from sanctioned tanker
events.description: On June 30, a Russian warship ordered Greenpeace activists to stay away while they were photographing a sanctioned crude oil tanker, the Kira K, in the Black Sea. The incident highlights ongoing tensions and enforcement of sanctions affecting maritime operations.

events.title: Pirates Attack Ships in Gulf of Aden
events.description: A pirate group boarded and damaged a vessel on July 1 in the Gulf of Aden, then pursued a second ship. Activity in the region remains elevated.

events.title: Oman's Role in Future of Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has been asserting control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, but analysis shows Oman's position and decisions will also significantly influence the strait's future and regional freight flows.

events.title: Iran escalates control claims over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran is escalating steps to assert control over the Strait of Hormuz, with strikes on transiting vessels and sites causing marine traffic disruptions. This comes amid ongoing US-Iran negotiations and rising oil volumes from Gulf states.

events.title: IATA Direct Air Waybill changes impact forwarders
events.description: IATA's revised Direct Air Waybill (DAWB) framework, effective immediately, alters contractual responsibilities between airlines, shippers, and forwarders, potentially increasing legal and insurance risks for freight forwarders. FIATA claims the industry review process was bypassed.

events.title: Foreign Ship Runs Aground in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A foreign container ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz after taking an 'unauthorized' route, according to Iran. The incident highlights ongoing tensions and risks in this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Russia imports gasoline from India to address shortages
events.description: Russia has begun importing gasoline from India via sea to address fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian attacks on its energy infrastructure.

events.title: US and Iran Hold Talks to Secure Hormuz Shipping
events.description: The U.S. and Iran held indirect technical talks in Doha to agree on the flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and work toward a lasting ceasefire.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz peace talks boost coal prices
events.description: Thermal coal prices fell as ongoing peace talks between the US and Iran raised hopes for a resolution and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Port of La Guaira operations suspended after earthquake
events.description: Commercial operations at the Port of La Guaira in Venezuela remain suspended following a recent earthquake. Authorities have not yet authorized the resumption of import and export activities, causing shipment delays.

events.title: Truck driver steals $2.9M cargo using fake docs
events.description: An Indiana truck driver was arrested for allegedly using fraudulent documents to steal $2.9 million worth of tungsten oxide powder in Pennsylvania. The stolen cargo was intended for shipment to Japan.

events.title: Maersk cautious on Middle East transits due to ongoing instability
events.description: Maersk is proceeding with caution regarding Middle East transits due to ongoing unpredictability, despite recent positive developments between the US and Iran. The company will resume transits only after confirming safety through thorough risk assessments.

events.title: Rising freight costs from Brazil to US impact trade
events.description: Ocean spot rates from Brazil to the US have surged by 50% for 40ft containers, driven by higher fuel costs and front-loading to avoid upcoming US tariffs. This increase signals potential trade flow disruptions despite weak underlying demand.

events.title: CBP enforces strict forced labor import rules
events.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection has issued new forced labor enforcement guidelines requiring full documentation of every supplier in a production chain. Failure to provide this visibility may result in denied entry for imported goods, significantly impacting logistics and import operations.

events.title: MSC invests in Vizhinjam Port, raising monopoly concerns
events.description: MSC has agreed to acquire a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port, part of Adani Ports, as the port undergoes a major expansion. This move raises concerns about market dominance and could influence freight flows in the region.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: The EU has terminated the de minimis exemption for low-value imports, imposing a €3 customs duty on parcels under €150. This policy change aims to curb the influx of low-cost goods, particularly from China, and may impact cross-border e-commerce and logistics flows.

events.title: MSC orders 20 new ultra-large container vessels
events.description: MSC has placed orders and options for 20 ultra-large container vessels with the Hengli yard in China, increasing its capacity lead over Maersk and expanding its already massive newbuilding pipeline.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts global crude flows
events.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz in Q2 2026 has significantly disrupted global crude oil flows, forcing the VLCC market to adapt and reroute shipments away from this critical oil chokepoint.

events.title: COSCO wins Tarragona terminal award
events.description: A COSCO Shipping Ports-led consortium has received an award notification to develop a new multipurpose terminal at the Port of Tarragona, Spain. The project, valued at around €144.6m, could expand COSCO's European port network.

events.title: Middle East Disruptions Impact Cargo Flows
events.description: Ongoing disruptions in the Middle East are affecting cargo flows and routing decisions, causing shifts in supply chain operations across Asia-Pacific trade corridors.

events.title: Bunker Fuel Availability Strain in Singapore
events.description: VLSFO bunker fuel availability in Singapore remains under strain, with lead times varying from nine days to over four weeks across suppliers.

events.title: MSC invests $1.4B for 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port
events.description: Adani Ports has entered into a definitive agreement with Terminal Investment Limited (TiL), part of MSC Group, for the sale of a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port for $1.4 billion. The investment signals a strategic industry move that could impact regional port operations and logistics flows.

events.title: Global Shipping Workforce Shortage Crisis
events.description: The global shipping industry is facing a deepening workforce shortage crisis, with a lack of certified personnel threatening operations and efficiency.

events.title: Acid spill disrupts Houston Ship Channel operations
events.description: A sulfuric acid spill at a terminal on the Houston Ship Channel led to OSHA fining the operator $82,000. The incident disrupted port operations and raised safety concerns.

events.title: Ukraine warns ships aiding Russia in conflict
events.description: Ukraine has issued a warning to ships aiding Russia in the conflict, responding to Russian allegations of terrorism following strikes on commercial shipping. The letter was addressed to the International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Pirates Hold Tanker Crew Off Somalia
events.description: A tanker's crew has been held by pirates off the coast of Somalia since early May. Egypt is intensifying efforts to secure their release.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Crisis Economic Impact
events.description: The UN warns that despite the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the over 100-day disruption has caused lasting economic damage to global shipping and energy markets.

events.title: Iran Asserts Control Over Hormuz Strait
events.description: Iran has escalated rhetoric about asserting control over maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, ahead of new talks in Qatar aimed at ending its conflict with the US.

events.title: US Retailers Accelerate China Orders Ahead of Tariff Hike
events.description: U.S. retailers are frontloading orders from China by four-to-six weeks to secure holiday inventory before expected tariff hikes later this year, according to shipping executives.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Disruption Affects LNG Trade
events.description: Shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war could stall global liquefied natural gas trade in 2026, according to Shell, though growth is expected to resume in 2027.

events.title: AIS Protocol Update in Kuwaiti Waters
events.description: As of 30 Jun 2026, vessels in Kuwaiti waters must maintain continuous AIS operation. This policy change may affect vessel tracking and compliance procedures for ships transiting the area.

events.title: Maersk seasonal blank sailing on Northern Star service
events.description: Maersk has announced a seasonal blank sailing on its Northern Star service due to seasonal cargo demand and market fluctuations. The affected voyage includes a southbound departure from Shanghai on 6 September 2026.

events.title: Maersk adjusts intermodal fuel fees in Europe
events.description: Maersk has updated its Intermodal Fuel Fee (EFS/IFS) levels across Europe due to ongoing high energy prices and Middle East security issues affecting fuel availability. The new fees apply from 6 July to 20 July 2026 and will be reviewed every two weeks.

events.title: Containers lost at sea nearly triple in 2025
events.description: A 2025 report found that 1,478 containers were lost at sea, nearly tripling from previous years, primarily due to challenging weather and vessel fires.

events.title: U.S. cargo airlines seek Venezuela flight approval
events.description: Atlas Air and ABX Air have requested U.S. government approval to start cargo flights to Venezuela, which has been restricted from commercial service. This could signal a potential policy shift affecting freight access to the region.

events.title: Oman clarifies stance on Strait of Hormuz amid Iran talks
events.description: Oman has clarified its position on the Strait of Hormuz amid widening talks with Iran, following recent exchanges of fire between the U.S. and Iran. The situation remains tense, with potential implications for global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: MSC revises freight rates from South Asia to Europe
events.location: South Asia to Europe shipping routes
events.description: MSC has announced updated Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for shipments from South Asia, including Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan, to Europe, effective 15 July 2026. The new rates are US$2,550 per 20DV and US$2,850 per 40DV/HC for Sri Lanka and Bangladesh.

events.title: COSCO consortium wins Tarragona terminal concession
events.description: A consortium led by COSCO SHIPPING Ports has been awarded a concession to develop a multipurpose terminal at the Port of Tarragona, Spain, following a public tender process.

events.title: Global Truck Driver Shortage Crisis
events.description: The global truck driver shortage, with nearly 3 million vacancies, is now a structural threat to freight transport, according to the International Road Transport Union. An aging workforce and growing demand are expected to worsen the crisis over the next five years.

events.title: Port congestion hits four-year high in North Asia
events.description: Container port congestion has reached a four-year high, with 3.7 million teu tied up globally. North Asian ports account for 38% of the congestion, leading to potential freight rate increases.

events.title: MSC gains stake in Vizhinjam port
events.description: MSC's terminal arm, TiL, acquires a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port for $2.85 billion, strengthening its partnership with APSEZ.

events.title: Hormuz closure impacts fuel costs and BAFs
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has increased fuel costs, leading to new BAFs effective 1 July. Carriers are passing on these costs to shippers, causing concerns over rising freight expenses.

events.title: Russia extends oil export ban linked to G7/EU price cap
events.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin extended the ban on oil and petroleum product exports subject to the G7 and EU price cap through the end of 2027. The move could further disrupt global oil markets and trade flows.

events.title: U.S.-Iran military exchanges raise Hormuz concerns
events.description: Fresh military exchanges between the U.S. and Iran have raised concerns over potential supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint. However, renewed diplomatic talks have tempered some of these fears, leading to a slight decline in oil prices.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Gradually Reopening
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is gradually reopening after disruptions, though freight rates continue to rise. Carriers are cancelling fewer sailings, indicating stabilizing schedules.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Tensions Persist Amid Peace Talks
events.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains steady, but ongoing tensions between Iran and the U.S. continue to cast uncertainty. Peace talks are set to resume in Doha, Qatar, with little clarity on a resolution.

events.title: Adani and MSC expand partnership at Vizhinjam Port
events.description: Adani Ports and MSC have agreed for Terminal Investment Limited to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Private Limited, with a total deal value of US$2.85 billion. The partnership aims to develop and operate the Vizhinjam port, a strategic deep-water port on India's southwest coast.

events.title: Carriers boost capacity on East Asia-Australia route
events.description: Shipping lines are increasing capacity on the East Asia-Australia trade lane due to a 30% surge in freight rates. CMA CGM and Cosco affiliate OOCL are launching a dedicated express service to meet rising demand and address tight capacity.

events.title: Increased Traffic in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has increased following Iran’s recent attacks on ships, with more crude tankers entering the Persian Gulf.

events.title: China Conducts Patrols Near Scarborough Shoal
events.description: China's military and coast guard conducted patrols around Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea, asserting territorial claims and potentially heightening regional tensions.

events.title: U.S. dry van spot rates surge 31% YoY
events.description: The U.S. Bank Freight Payment Index shows dry van spot rates surged 31% year-over-year in May 2026 due to tightening capacity, signaling significant cost increases in freight transportation.

events.title: Russian LNG carrier armed with machine guns in Baltic Sea
events.description: A Russian LNG carrier operating in the Baltic Sea has been spotted with heavy machine guns mounted on board, marking an unusual militarization of a commercial vessel. The action raises concerns about the security implications for maritime trade routes in the region.

events.title: MSC acquires stake in Vizhinjam port
events.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) has agreed to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Ports' Vizhinjam transhipment terminal in Kerala, India, for $1.4 billion. The investment signals MSC's growing commitment to India's container market and port infrastructure.

events.title: Yanbu Red Sea bypass remains active
events.description: The Red Sea bypass route through Yanbu in Saudi Arabia is operating at near-peak capacity as Gulf terminals reopen, with ongoing toll claims from Tehran and Muscat suggesting the alternative route may remain in use.

events.title: BNSF Expands Barstow Intermodal Hub
events.description: BNSF has won approval for a $4 billion intermodal facility in Barstow, aiming to shift container sorting away from Los Angeles. The project raises questions about costs, logistics efficiency, and potential impacts on freight flows.

events.title: Iran Conflict Drives Up BAF and Shipping Costs
events.description: The escalation of the Iran conflict has caused fuel-related charges (BAF) to more than double, with carriers imposing emergency bunker surcharges. Shippers now face increased transportation costs and the risk of being charged twice for fuel price increases.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz operational capacity severely reduced
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains open but is operating at about 10% of pre-war levels due to two kinetic incidents, a suspended IMO evacuation corridor, and ongoing disruptions. Commercial traffic is significantly constrained, impacting global freight flows.

events.title: AG Oil Flows Normalization Progress
events.description: Following a ceasefire on 24 June, temporary routing arrangements coordinated by Oman and the IMO have enabled a gradual resumption of commercial shipping in a previously contested transit regime.

events.title: Cargo ship taking on water along Indian Coast
events.description: A small cargo vessel along the Indian Coast reported taking on water in multiple compartments. The Indian Coast Guard provided assistance to the vessel.

events.title: SC Ports suspends Leatherman Terminal operations
events.location: Hugh K. Leatherman Terminal, South Carolina
events.description: South Carolina Ports will temporarily pause container operations at the Hugh K. Leatherman Terminal starting August 1 due to ongoing trade challenges and poor performance since the terminal's opening over five years ago.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transit uncertainty after attacks
events.description: Ships continued to transit the Strait of Hormuz, but some owners reduced their visibility due to recent attacks, raising concerns about navigation safety in the critical oil shipping corridor.

events.title: Russian LNG Vessel Armed with Machine Guns
events.description: Estonian surveillance detected machine guns on a Russian LNG vessel, marking a potential escalation in the militarization of commercial shipping.

events.title: Maersk shifts SoCal imports to UP from BNSF
events.description: Maersk is shifting import containers moving inland from Southern California to Union Pacific (UP) from BNSF, signaling a major rail route adjustment affecting freight flows.

events.title: Top container line invests in Hamburg terminal
events.description: A top-five container line is acquiring a stake in Hamburg's Eurogate terminal, signaling a strategic industry move that could influence European port operations and logistics flows.

events.title: Oman hints at potential fees for ships through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Omani officials have informed European authorities that ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz may face charges, as returning to pre-war conditions is not feasible. The statement signals potential policy changes affecting freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Intra-Asia container freight rates drop 4%
events.description: Drewry’s Intra-Asia Container Index (IACI) fell 4% to $1,074 per 40ft container, with notable declines on routes from Shanghai to Manila, Jawaharlal Nehru Port, and Laem Chabang. While rates have eased, they remain near double pre-conflict levels.

events.title: Dry bulk Hormuz Strait transits rebound
events.description: Dry bulk vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have reached 100% of pre-war levels, with 62 verified transits reported on June 24, the highest single-day count since the conflict began.

events.title: Oman proposes Hormuz Strait fee plan
events.description: Oman is proposing a fee plan for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, raising questions about who controls access and charges for this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: CEVA launches Hanoi-Chicago air cargo charter
events.description: CEVA Logistics has introduced a new transpacific air cargo charter service between Hanoi and Chicago, operating three times a week to enhance freight capacity between Asia and the U.S.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz surcharge due to transit uncertainty
events.description: Uncertainty in the Strait of Hormuz has prompted carriers like Hapag-Lloyd to introduce new Middle East Emergency Surcharges (MES) for container shipments from India to the Persian Gulf due to alternative routing and operational adjustments.

events.title: US supplier sues container cartel for price manipulation
events.description: US supplier Atlantic Coast Container has filed a lawsuit against four container manufacturers indicted by the DOJ for allegedly colluding to restrict container output and inflate prices.

events.title: Iranian attack on Evergreen vessel near Hormuz
events.description: An Iranian attack on an Evergreen vessel occurred near the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting a key global shipping chokepoint. This follows a brief respite where some ships managed to leave the Persian Gulf after months of delays.

events.title: Shipping lines shift to regional ports over mega-hubs
events.description: Shipping lines are restructuring global service networks, favoring regional ports over major Asian transshipment hubs, according to Sea-Intelligence analysis of UNCTAD data.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transit authority dispute
events.description: Tehran is asserting control over ship movements in the Strait of Hormuz, creating uncertainty and prompting supply chain managers to consider overland alternatives.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz attacks disrupt LNG supply
events.description: A series of attacks in the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted the flow of liquefied natural gas, prompting Pakistan to urgently seek LNG deliveries.

events.title: Ship attacks disrupt Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Middle East producers continue oil and LNG loadings despite fresh ship attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and renewed U.S.-Iran strikes, raising concerns over regional supply chain stability.

events.title: Russia Arms LNG Tanker in Baltic, Escalating Tensions
events.description: Russia has reportedly armed a civilian LNG tanker in the Baltic Sea, according to Estonian border authorities. Analysts describe this as an unprecedented militarization of a Russian energy vessel, heightening maritime tensions with NATO.

events.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, following reports of Iranian strikes targeting Bahrain. The crew was safe, and no environmental damage was reported.

events.title: Mines Remain in Strait of Hormuz Shipping Lanes
events.description: The UN's International Maritime Organization estimates 80 mines remain in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route. The presence of mines has led to the creation of alternative shipping channels to ensure safe passage.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transits rise amid container rate surge
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz increased amid ongoing tensions, while Asia-US container shipping rates surged due to importers accelerating cargo ahead of potential new tariffs.

events.title: Scorpio Tankers resumes Hormuz Strait transit for jet fuel delivery
events.description: Scorpio Tankers has resumed transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with an LR2 tanker chartered to deliver jet fuel to Europe for $10 million. This marks the first direct clean tanker spot fixture following recent disruptions.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new FAK rates and surcharges
events.description: CMA CGM has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates and Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) across multiple trade lanes, effective from 5 July to 25 July 2026. The changes include new FAK rates from the Mediterranean to the Middle East and Red Sea starting 6 July 2026.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis tests shipmanagers' trust
events.description: The ongoing crisis in the Strait of Hormuz is creating direct challenges for shipmanagers, highlighting risks to global trade and freight flows beyond typical disruptions.

events.title: Gunsan Shipyard resumes vessel production after 9-year hiatus
events.description: Gunsan Shipyard, one of South Korea's largest dormant shipbuilding facilities, has secured its first newbuilding contract, signaling a potential return to full production. This development could help alleviate capacity constraints in the shipbuilding sector amid high demand.

events.title: MSC orders 20 new LNG dual-fuel megaships
events.description: Mediterranean Shipping Co (MSC) has ordered up to 20 ultra-large LNG dual-fuel container ships from Hengli Heavy Industry in China, signaling a major industry move toward larger, greener vessels.

events.title: US and Iran exchange strikes near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US and Iran exchanged fresh military strikes over the weekend, escalating tensions and undermining the fragile ceasefire around the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Liner networks shift from Asia's mega hubs
events.description: Container shipping carriers are shifting away from Asia's major transhipment hubs to smaller regional ports, marking a significant change in global freight routing strategies.

events.title: Tanker Struck by Drone Off Oman Coast
events.description: A Panama-flagged tanker was struck by a drone off the coast of Oman on June 27, amid ongoing tensions in the region. The U.S. responded with new strikes, raising concerns about disruptions to maritime trade in the critical Gulf shipping corridor.

events.title: Santos Port deepens navigation channel to 16m
events.description: Santos Port Authority awarded a US$105 million contract to deepen the navigation channel from 15 to 16 metres, starting in 2026. The project aims to accommodate larger vessels and improve freight flow efficiency.

events.title: CMA CGM Containership Uses Iranian Route Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A CMA CGM containership has taken an Iranian route through the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a potential shift in shipping lanes amid ongoing regional tensions and safety concerns.

events.title: Turkish-owned vessel beached in Kenya for six weeks
events.description: A Turkish-owned vessel has been stranded near a marine protected area in Lamu, Kenya, for six weeks. Salvage operations are underway to remove the ship and mitigate environmental and navigational risks.

events.title: TVL Marine launches Hong Kong–Taiwan feeder service
events.description: TVL Marine has re-entered the container shipping market with a new feeder service connecting Hong Kong to ports in Taiwan, including Keelung, Taichung, and Kaohsiung. The service is operated by a single 1,000 TEU vessel.

events.title: ZIM adds Pisco to ZCX service route
events.description: ZIM Integrated Shipping Services has expanded its ZIM Colibri Express (ZCX) service to include a call at Pisco, Peru, adjusting the route between the US East Coast and West Coast of South America.

events.title: Russian Warship Presence in English Channel
events.description: The Russian frigate RFS Admiral Grigorovich has been lingering in the English Channel since late April, likely to escort dark fleet tankers, raising concerns over potential disruptions to maritime traffic and security.

events.title: Vancouver Fraser Port approves narrows dredging
events.description: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority has received regulatory approval to begin dredging near Second Narrows in Burrard Inlet, starting in September. The project aims to increase port capacity and improve supply chain efficiency by allowing larger vessel loads at the Westridge terminal.

events.title: SM Line removes Yantian from PNS service
events.description: SM Line has revised its Transpacific PNS service, removing the call at Shenzhen (Yantian) and introducing a new feeder connection, the Shenzhen–Busan Express (SBX), to handle transshipment between the two ports.

events.title: Port of Brownsville completes $295M ship channel deepening
events.description: The Port of Brownsville has completed a $295 million ship channel deepening project, enhancing its capacity to accommodate larger vessels and improving freight flow efficiency along the U.S.-Mexico border.

events.title: Loconi builds new intermodal terminal in Poland
events.description: Loconi Intermodal has begun construction of a new intermodal terminal in Zbąszynek, Poland, adjacent to the E20 railway line. The terminal is part of the company's expansion in Central and Eastern Europe.

events.title: Tanker struck in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. strikes Iran
events.description: A tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting U.S. military strikes against Iran, escalating tensions and risking disruption to a key global shipping route.

events.title: Asia building maritime redundancy around China
events.description: Asian middle powers are developing alternative maritime routes and infrastructure to reduce dependence on China's dominant shipping corridors, enhancing supply chain redundancy.

events.title: Sweden Arms Coast Guard Amid Baltic Tensions
events.description: Sweden is fitting machine guns to its civilian coast guard vessels due to rising tensions with Russia, which could impact maritime security and freight flows in the Baltic region.

events.title: Tanker Struck by Drone off Oman
events.description: A Panama-flagged tanker was struck by a drone off the coast of Oman on June 27, amid rising tensions with Iran, which has claimed to be firing warning shots in the region.

events.title: Aluminum cargo released from Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Vitol Group has successfully shipped a stranded cargo of aluminum out of the Strait of Hormuz, indicating potential supply relief for buyers after a period of disruption.

events.title: Saudi Arabia ramps up oil exports as Gulf ports reopen
events.description: Saudi Arabia is increasing crude oil exports as its Persian Gulf ports, previously closed due to the Iran war, resume operations. The reopening is helping restore regional freight flows.

events.title: Tanker Struck in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was struck in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting naval authorities to raise the threat level for ships in the region, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Second Tanker Struck in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A second merchant vessel has been struck in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting escalating security risks and disrupting critical global shipping routes between the Persian Gulf and the Arabian Sea.

events.title: Container Rates Near Two-Year High
events.description: Containerized freight rates are approaching a two-year high due to renewed concerns over potential tariffs, disrupting global shipping expectations for 2026.

events.title: US-Iran Strait of Hormuz Agreement Extension
events.description: The US and Iran signed a 14-point memorandum extending a 60-day agreement to de-escalate tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: RCL joins Far East–Red Sea container service
events.description: Regional Container Lines (RCL) has joined the Far East–Red Sea RCS/RCX service operated by Global Feeder Shipping and T.S. Lines, marketing it under the name RCR01. The service deploys seven vessels and operates between major Chinese ports and the Red Sea.

events.title: Hormuz Strait faces asymmetric threats
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is experiencing heightened tension as asymmetric threats, including sea mines and unmanned systems, are being deployed, impacting global shipping and energy flows.

events.title: U.S. Airstrike on Iran Following Strait of Hormuz Attack
events.description: U.S. forces conducted an airstrike on Iranian territory in retaliation for an Iranian attack on a Taiwanese container ship, the Ever Lovely, in the Strait of Hormuz. The incident raises concerns about regional stability and potential disruptions to global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: ZIM revises ZMP service, adds Tianjin
events.description: ZIM Integrated Shipping Services has updated its Mediterranean–Far East ZMP service to include a call at Tianjin, removing Busan from the rotation. Busan will now be served via transshipment at Ningbo.

events.title: Transpacific container rates rise 12% weekly
events.description: Container spot rates on the transpacific Asia-North America west coast trade increased by 12% week-on-week, with the Shanghai-Los Angeles leg reaching $5,750 per 40ft container, 54% higher than the same week in 2025.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic normalizes
events.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is normalizing, reducing concerns over Middle East supply risks and easing pressure on European natural gas prices.

events.title: Surging Hormuz Shipments Restore Exports
events.description: Shipping transits through the Strait of Hormuz have accelerated, restoring Persian Gulf crude oil exports to about 75% of prewar levels following progress toward a US-Iran peace deal.

events.title: Houston Bunker Demand Declines
events.description: Bunker fuel demand in the port of Houston has softened, particularly for VLSFO, according to a trader. Recommended lead times for VLSFO and HSFO are now 6-7 days.

events.title: Cargo ship attacked near Oman
events.description: A cargo ship was attacked near Oman, raising concerns about regional security, though easing traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and U.S.-Iran peace optimism have offset some fears.

events.title: War-driven decline in tanker flows
events.description: Tanker flows dropped sharply in late March due to wartime disruptions, reaching a low of 3.31M barrels on 21 May before recovering to 6.97M barrels by 24 June.

events.title: Hormuz Strait maritime traffic resumes
events.description: Maritime traffic through the Hormuz Strait is expected to resume following a disruption, impacting global energy markets and supply chain flows for oil and gas.

events.title: Iranian Attack Adds Uncertainty to Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is seeing increased oil traffic, but an Iranian attack has introduced uncertainty and potential disruption to this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: SC Ports suspends Leatherman Terminal operations
events.description: South Carolina Ports will temporarily pause container operations at Leatherman Terminal starting August to address lower cargo volumes and an uncertain trade outlook. Operations will shift to Wando and North Charleston terminals.

events.title: Bourbon shipment stolen via carrier impersonation
events.description: A $500,000 shipment of Noble Oak Bourbon was stolen when suspects impersonated authorized carriers to pick up the freight. The incident highlights a growing cargo theft tactic involving impersonation and legitimate shipment information.

events.title: Iran accused of drone attack on ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating a U.S. ceasefire by conducting a drone attack on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Oman Considers Fees for Hormuz Strait Transit
events.description: Oman has informed European officials that the pre-war status quo for the Strait of Hormuz cannot be restored, and ships transiting the strait may be required to pay fees, signaling a potential policy shift affecting global shipping.

events.title: Iran reasserts control over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran reasserted its right to control shipping in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a ship near Oman, raising concerns about regional stability and maritime security.

events.title: 80 Mines Estimated in Hormuz Shipping Lanes
events.description: The International Maritime Organization estimates there are approximately 80 mines in the Strait of Hormuz's shipping lanes, significantly complicating the return of normal operations in this critical oil transport channel.

events.title: Yemen tensions threaten Red Sea shipping
events.description: Rising tensions between the Houthis and the Internationally Recognized Government in Yemen are causing concern for Red Sea shipping routes, with potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: Increase in containers lost at sea in 2025
events.description: The World Shipping Council reported 1,478 containers were lost at sea in 2025, up from 576 in 2024. While representing just 0.0005% of global container traffic, the increase highlights ongoing vessel-related disruptions in global freight flows.

events.title: South Carolina Ports suspends container terminal operations
events.description: South Carolina Ports has temporarily shut down a key container terminal due to trade uncertainty and cost-cutting measures, disrupting freight operations at the Port of Charleston.

events.title: Drone Strike on Ever Lovely in Strait
events.description: The Singapore-flagged containership Ever Lovely was struck by an unknown projectile on its starboard side on 25 June 2026, raising concerns about security and safety in the Strait of Malacca.

events.title: Lufthansa Cargo upgrades Frankfurt hub
events.description: Lufthansa Cargo has launched the first phase of a €600 million upgrade to its cargo hub at Frankfurt Airport, aiming to modernize operations and increase efficiency.

events.title: Transpacific spot freight rates hit 22-month high
events.description: Spot freight rates for 40ft containers from Shanghai to Los Angeles rose 12% to $5,750, contributing to a 5% weekly increase in the Drewry World Container Index to $4,166, its highest in 22 months.

events.title: Box ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The containership Ever Lovely was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to paused evacuation operations and conflicting claims about the vessel's transit status. The IMO has called for safety guarantees for vessels in the area.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic slows after ship attack
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz slowed following an attack on a Taiwanese-operated ship by Iran, according to ship tracking data. Fewer vessels transited the strait on Friday compared to earlier in the week.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Boosts Panama Canal Traffic
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has driven increased ship traffic through the Panama Canal, leading to higher-than-expected revenue forecasts for the canal in fiscal 2026.

events.title: EU extends economic sanctions against Russia
events.description: The European Council has extended economic sanctions against Russia for another year, until 31 July 2027, in response to the ongoing war in Ukraine. This policy move is likely to continue affecting global supply chains, particularly in energy, freight, and trade flows.

events.title: Vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A commercial vessel was struck in the Strait of Hormuz, causing a temporary halt in oil market sell-off and highlighting ongoing risks in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz evacuation paused after cargo vessel attack
events.description: The International Maritime Organization has suspended its evacuation operation in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a cargo vessel in the Gulf of Oman, raising concerns about maritime security in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz vessel traffic surges 48%
events.description: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose sharply this week, with 31 confirmed passages by mid-morning, representing a 48% increase from the previous day, according to Windward.

events.title: UK detains Russia-linked oil tanker Smyrtos
events.description: The 106,800 dwt Russia-linked oil tanker Smyrtos was seized off southern England by UK forces, marking the first UK-led interdiction of a shadow fleet tanker. Russia has warned of legal action if the cargo is sold, escalating tensions around enforcement of sanctions.

events.title: Evergreen containership hit near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An Evergreen containership was struck shortly after transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing the International Maritime Organization to pause its evacuation operation for vessels in the Gulf. The incident raises concerns about ongoing disruptions in this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Evergreen container ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The 9,532 teu Evergreen containership Ever Lovely was struck shortly after transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing the International Maritime Organization to pause its evacuation operation for vessels in the Gulf.

events.title: France intercepts Russian shadow fleet tanker near Sicily
events.description: French navy boarded the Russia-linked tanker Deliver near Sicily as part of efforts to disrupt Moscow's shadow fleet circumventing Western sanctions. The incident highlights ongoing disruptions in global energy freight flows.

events.title: Shipyard engine shortages curb output
events.description: A shortage of main engines, particularly dual-fuel low-speed two-stroke engines, is limiting shipyard output, echoing constraints from the 2007 shipbuilding boom. This shortage is creating bottlenecks in the shipbuilding supply chain.

events.title: Charleston suspends new terminal operations due to low volume
events.description: South Carolina Ports has paused operations at its new terminal in Charleston, citing low cargo volumes and high operating costs. The decision may impact regional port efficiency and freight handling capacity.

events.title: Containers Lost Overboard Rise in 2024
events.description: The number of containers lost overboard increased for the second consecutive year, with incidents contributing to the fourth-highest level on record, raising concerns about maritime safety and cargo loss.

events.title: KONGSBERG upgrades Great Belt VTS traffic system
events.description: KONGSBERG has delivered a new Vessel Traffic Services (VTS) system for the Great Belt in Denmark, enhancing traffic management through one of Europe's busiest waterways.

events.title: Ship Attack Off Oman Disrupts IMO Evacuation
events.description: A merchant vessel was attacked in the Gulf of Oman, leading the International Maritime Organization to suspend its coordinated evacuation of ships stranded in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: KONGSBERG upgrades Great Belt VTS traffic management
events.description: KONGSBERG has delivered an advanced Vessel Traffic Services solution for Great Belt VTS in Denmark, enhancing traffic management through one of Europe’s busiest straits.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes, easing oil supply concerns
events.description: Improved traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has eased concerns over Middle East crude supply disruptions, contributing to a fourth consecutive session of falling oil prices.

events.title: EU Proposes Sanctions on Vessels Aiding Russian Shadow Fleet
events.description: The EU proposed its 21st sanctions package against Russia, including targeting vessels that support the Russian shadow fleet by providing services like bunkering. The sanctions aim to disrupt Russian trade and energy flows.

events.title: Containership attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.location: South of Oman, near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A containership was struck by a projectile south of Oman near the eastern exit of the Strait of Hormuz. The International Maritime Organization has paused evacuations in the area amid the incident.

events.title: Gas Tanker Collides with Fishing Trawler Off South Korea
events.description: A small gas tanker collided with a fishing trawler off the coast of Ulsan, South Korea, prompting a search and rescue operation by the Ulsan Coast Guard on June 25.

events.title: Charleston Terminal Suspends Operations Due to Low Volume
events.description: South Carolina Ports has suspended operations at its new terminal in Charleston, citing low cargo volumes and high operating costs.

events.title: ILWU strike against California sugar processor
events.description: International Longshore and Warehouse Union workers have initiated the first strike in decades against a major California sugar processor, potentially disrupting freight and logistics operations.

events.title: Attack on cargo vessel in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An unidentified cargo vessel was the target of a suspected attack off Oman as shipping resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Containership Struck Off Oman Coast Amid Hormuz Warnings
events.description: A Singapore-flagged containership was struck by an unknown projectile off the coast of Oman shortly after Iran's IRGC renewed Hormuz Strait transit warnings, raising concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade.

events.title: France Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
events.description: France's navy seized a tanker linked to Russia's 'shadow fleet,' intensifying European efforts to enforce sanctions against Russian oil exports.

events.title: Port Qasim offices closed for Ashura holiday
events.description: Port Qasim Authority offices in Pakistan are closed on June 25-26, 2026, for the Ashura holiday, potentially affecting port operations and administrative processes.

events.title: Iran-Oman Tensions Impact Gulf Shipping Exit Plan
events.description: Tensions between Iran and Oman have emerged as the IMO coordinates a plan to manage the exit of ships trapped in the Gulf, raising concerns about potential disruptions to maritime traffic.

events.title: French Forces Detain Sanctioned Tanker
events.description: French forces detained a sanctioned tanker linked to the shadow fleet off the coast of Sicily on June 23, as part of ongoing enforcement actions against illicit oil trade.

events.title: Port of Long Beach imports surge 40%
events.description: The Port of Long Beach experienced a 40% surge in imports in May, setting a record for cargo growth amid ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty.

events.title: Europe extends Middle East flight restrictions
events.description: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has renewed its advisory against flying over Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon, forcing European airlines to take longer routes and increasing operating costs, despite improved aviation activity in the Gulf.

events.title: Arctic shipping route interest rises amid risks
events.description: Melting sea ice and geopolitical disruptions are driving interest in Arctic shipping routes between Asia and Europe. However, Allianz Commercial warns of high risks, including sanctions, geopolitical tensions, and environmental challenges.

events.title: Saudi Oil Terminal Resumes Loading After Conflict
events.description: Saudi Arabia is restarting crude oil loading at the Ras Tanura terminal in the Persian Gulf, signaling a resumption of Middle Eastern oil supplies following the Iran war.

events.title: Ships Turn Back in Strait of Hormuz Over IRGC Warnings
events.description: Merchant ships are reportedly turning back in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) renews warnings, raising concerns over potential disruptions to global shipping.

events.title: China-India container rates surge due to strong demand
events.description: Container freight rates from China to India have risen by 25-30% since late May due to strong demand from Indian importers, despite increased capacity. Spot rates from Shanghai to Nhava Sheva are now around $2,300 per TEU.

events.title: IMO unveils Hormuz evacuation plan, vessels transit
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) published a Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan, prompting increased vessel transits through the region. Maersk shipped two vessels through the strait in coordination with security measures.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes, easing oil supply concerns
events.description: Oil prices fell to pre-Iran war levels as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz normalized, reducing concerns over Middle East crude supply disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz MoU and Joint Working Group
events.description: A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between Iran and the United States, and a Joint Working Group was established between Iran and Oman to improve maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz crisis reshapes maritime governance
events.description: The ongoing Hormuz crisis is altering global maritime governance as Iran's tactics influence shipping behavior, with vessels no longer reverting to previous norms once adjustments are made.

events.title: Maersk Baltimore exits Persian Gulf
events.description: Maersk confirmed that its vessel Maersk Baltimore and a time-chartered vessel have safely exited the Persian Gulf after a successful transit through the Strait of Hormuz following detailed security assessments.

events.title: Severe global shortage of shipping officers by 2030
events.description: The shipping industry faces a critical shortage of over 113,000 STCW-certified officers by 2030, threatening the operation of the global merchant fleet, according to a report by BIMCO and the International Chamber of Shipping.

events.title: Experienced Docker Dies in Mooring Line Incident
events.description: An experienced docker was killed in a mooring line incident at a ferry terminal in Cairnryan, Scotland, raising concerns about safety in port operations.

events.title: IRGC Rejects Omani Safe-Passage Plan for Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy has rejected Oman's proposed safe transit plan for the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about potential disruptions to one of the world's most critical shipping chokepoints.

events.title: IMO evacuation plan for stranded seafarers in Middle East
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has launched an evacuation plan for approximately 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Middle East since late February. The International Chamber of Shipping (ICS) has welcomed the initiative, emphasizing the priority of safely evacuating the affected crew.

events.title: Shadow VLCC Era resurfaces under Comoros flag
events.description: The 2002-built VLCC Era, part of the shadow fleet with a history of identity changes, has resurfaced under the Comoros flag. The vessel, previously known as Marinera and Bella 1, is now being tracked under a new identity, raising questions about its operational status and potential implications for the shadow fleet.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic shows recovery signs
events.description: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is increasing, signaling a gradual return to normal operations after months of disruption, though the waterway remains under strict restrictions and many seafarers are still stranded.

events.title: CMA CGM introduces hazardous cargo surcharge on key routes
events.location: Far East to Indian Ocean, South Africa, East Africa
events.description: CMA CGM has announced a new Hazardous Surcharge of US$500 per TEU for hazardous cargo shipped from the Far East to the Indian Ocean, South Africa, and East Africa, effective 1 July 2026.
